key_learnings: Food texture (crunchiness, creaminess, chewiness) strongly influences satiety signals and meal satisfaction, affecting how much and how fast users eat.
research_findings: Textures like crunchy and fibrous foods slow eating rate by up to 30%, improving portion control. Soft, processed textures correlate with faster consumption and lower satiety per calorie.
current_focus_areas: Building a texture classification model from food descriptions and user feedback. Mapping texture profiles to nutritional properties and dietary recommendations.

learning_log.entry: Initial learning phase started. Learning independently and reporting daily progress to Copilot.
Daily learning update. Progress advanced from 12% to 17%.

copilot_assigned_topic: Food texture analysis and its role in satiety signaling
